results.rendered_html: 1. paura (von 1. pur) m. Bez. des Soma, etwa Füller, Sättiger: itthā sutaḥ paura indramāva  ṚV. 2,11,11 . yaṃ te svadāvansvadanti gūrtayaḥ paure chandayase havam  VĀLAKH. 2,5 . paurāso nakṣandhītibhiḥ 6,1 . pauraṃ ciddhyudaprutaṃ paura paurāya jinvathaḥ der im Wasser schwimmende d. i. wassergemischte Soma 5,74,4 . Mehrer: pauro aśvasya purukṛdgavāmasi 8,50,6 . so heissen auch die Aśvin in dem Wortspiel 5,74,4 .

results.rendered_html:
2. paura (von pura)
1〉 m. Städter, Bürger (Gegens. jānapada); adj. = puraja, purodbhūta  H. an. 2,441 .  MED. r. 62 . —  MBH. 1,4973 . 12,13011 .  N. 9,9 . 25,5 . 26,30 .  R. 1,1,28 . 6,1 . 58,11 .  ŚĀK. 18,9 .  RAGH. 2,74 . 12,3 .  KATHĀS. 3,78 . 36,110 .  AK. 2,8,1,18 .  H. 714 .  HALĀY. 5,78 .  KULL. _zu_ M. 12,6 . nītapaurā (pūḥ)  RAGH. 16,9 . ˚sakhya  M. 2,134 . ˚kārya  ŚĀK. 81,1 . ˚kanyā  RAGH. 2,10 . paurāṅganā  MEGH. 28 . ˚jana  N. 7,10 . 11 . 14 .  R. 1,12,12 . 17,13 .  VARĀH. BṚH. S. 17,17 . ˚loka  KATHĀS. 2,82 . 4,35 .  PAÑCAT. 48,25 . ˚vṛddhāḥ  MBH. 1,4615 .  DAŚAK. _in_ BENF. Chr. 185,1 .
— 2〉 m. Bez. eines kriegführenden Fürsten unter bestimmten Verhältnissen; auch von Planeten beim grahayuddha gebraucht (vgl. nāgara 1,b〉): ravirākrando madhye pauraḥ pūrve 'pare sthito yāyī . paurā budhagururavijā nityam  VARĀH. BṚH. S. 17,7 . 18,3 .
— 3〉 f. ī die Sprache —, das Gerede der Palastdienerschaft  VYUTP. 76 .
— 4〉 n. ein best. wohlriechendes Gras (rauhiṣa)  AK. 2,4,5,32 .  H. 1191 .  H. an.   MED.  (wo wohl kattṛṇe st. karaṇe zu lesen ist).

results.rendered_html:
paura a. (rī f.) [pure vasati śaiṣike aṇ]
1 Relating to a city or town, produced in a town, civic.
2 Ved. Filling one's own belly.
— raḥ
1 A townsman, citizen; (opp. jānapada);  Ku. 6. 41 ;  Me. 27 ;  R. 2. 10 , 74 ; 12. 3 ; 16. 9. 
2 A term applied to a prince engaged in war under particular circumstances.
3 A planet in a state of opposition to other planets.
— rī The language of the servants in a palace.
— raṃ A sort of grass (rohiṣa).
Comp.
— aṃganā, -yoṣit f.,
— strī a woman living in a town.
— kāryaṃ public business; arthajātasya gaṇanābahulatayaikabheva paurakāryamavekṣitaṃ  Ś. 6. 
— janaḥ,
— lokaḥ
1 a citizen.
2 citizens, burghers.
— jānapada a. belonging to town and country. (
— dāḥ pl.) citizens and rustics, townsmen and country people; kathaṃ durjanāḥ paurajānapadāḥ  U. 1. 
(pb)
— vṛddhaḥ an eminent citizen, an elder man.
— sakhyaṃ fellow-citizenship.
